title: ‘Star Wars: The Last Jedi’ Is Just the Beginning for Rian Johnson | The Big Picture (Ep. 41)
format: A quick bonus interview: Sean Fennessy talks to Rian Johnson about making The Last Jedi, the character he couldn't wait to write, and the trilogy he's signed up for next.

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: Johnson's own version of what Star Wars felt like growing up. He wanted buoyancy even in the darker, island-bound stretches.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: The one he had toys for as a kid, his first exposure and the one that stuck with him most.

films.title: The Empire Strikes Back
films.note: Got more into it as an adult; the Vader reveal is the model for the moral complexity he chased.

films.title: Return of the Jedi
films.note: He was 10 when it came out, the ideal age for it.

films.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
films.note: Left him with the puzzle of why Luke exiled himself.

films.title: Twelve O'Clock High
films.note: Gregory Peck reference point for the Poe/Holdo authority clash.

films.title: The Dawn Patrol
films.note: British counterpart, with David Niven, same hotshot-pilot-vs-command trope.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: His answer to “last great thing you've seen”: Paul Thomas Anderson is his favorite filmmaker working today.

films.title: The Master
films.note: Cited alongside Phantom Thread as work that lives in his head.
